calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello & Richard Shu for (Administrator) Barbara Simon. Shelley Feinberg (via court call) as former counsel for the former administrator.

The Court stated that there are issues regarding fees, that there are no time reports submitted by Ms. Simon regarding her request for extraordinary fees for tax returns and related issues, whether the work and amount requested are reasonable and appropriate in regards to Ms. Feinberg as well as Ms. Robello's firm. Ms. Robello addressed Ms. Simon's request stating that there were tax issues, that Ms. Simon is a tax agent and dealt with the taxes herself rather than sending them out to be done, that her bills are reasonable and not part of the administration fee. The Court stated that her request may have been reasonable, that she needs to tell the court what she did and how much time it took and that the court has asked her and she refuses to tell. Ms. Robello stated that Ms. Simon has done this on other cases and it has been accepted by the court. The Court stated that it has an obligation to examine this information, that it raises a red flag that Ms. Simon will not provide the information and that it will give 1 more chance to provide the information or will have to make a determination based on what it has been given. Ms. Robello addressed the extraordinary fees to her firm stating that they had been in close contact with the beneficiaries, that they believe there was no agreement with the nephew regarding the home, that they talked at the settlement conference, that they were given periodic updates and that they had to review several contradictory letters from the nephew. The Court asked if the beneficiaries were kept aware of the litigation costs and that it would come out of the estate. Ms. Robello stated that they were given updates at each stage, that they were told of the position of the opponent and that they requested to go forward and that they had a cordial relationship. The Court stated that there is some concern regarding the use of multiple attorneys for the trial, that some of the pleadings took a long time to draft and that it seems that Ms. Feinberg did quite a lot of investigative work and then Ms. Robello's firm did the same. Ms. Robello stated that the amount of time duplicated at trial was 9.5 hours, that other hours were divided between different things, that certain witnesses were handled by co-counsel but prepped by Ms. Robello, that there were issues regarding discovery and Ms. Feinberg interviewed clients regarding claims, medical records and interviews with witnesses and that there were new documents created by the claimant. The Court addressed Ms. Feinberg stating that at some point she discovered malfeasance and what is the justification to paying for subsequent work and work regarding the litigation on the real estate. Ms. Feinberg stated that she wanted to make sure the case was clear enough for Promissory Estoppel, that there was a lot of investigation, declarations of witnesses, that it pointed to a strong case, that the beneficiaries were informed and urged to settle, that she was as thorough as possible, that she obtained photographs and testimony, did analysis and sent it to the administrator, prepared the Inventory & Appraisal and then discovered funds were being taken and at that point had to bring in another firm and supply them with the documents. Ms. Robello stated that in the transition they were given a draft ex parte, then her firm drafted two ex parte applications, and 850 petition and a double damages claim. The accounting petition was taken under submission as of 11/10/15 subject to receipt of a filed supplement.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Deborah Fox for Barbara Simon. Marc Seidenfeld for Farid Sharif.

Mr. Seidenfeld stated that he is willing to waive any potential conflict of interest issue. Ms. Fox explained there was limited scope representation of Lubna in the beginning in order to freeze the assets because the Beneficiaries were the only Parties that were able to do so. Counsel indicated a two to three day trial and stated that they need time for discovery. The Petition to Establish Petitioner's Claim to Ownership of Real Property, etc. was continued to April 2, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and trail setting.

Off Calendar Oct-16-2013 Continued To Jan-22-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marc Seidenfeld for Farid Sharif. Kevin Lachona for Barbara Simon (Administrator).

Mr. Seidenfeld stated that they agreed to mediate their dispute. The Court directed Counsel to send notice of acknowledgment of receipt to the two Nieces (Lubna and Farida). The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than January 17, 2014. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by January 17, 2014. The Petition to Establish Claim to Ownership of Real Property, etc. was continued to and the Parties are to return on January 22, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Petition To Establish Petitioners Claim To Ownership Of Real Property Now In Possession Of Decedents Estate And For Order Directing Administrator Of Estate To Convey Said Real Property To Petitioner
Off Calendar Sep-30-2013 Continued To Oct-16-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing To Determine The Amount Of Double Damages
Off Calendar Oct-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Deborah Fox for Barbara Simon (Administrator).

Ms. Fox stated that two of the Heirs do not wish to pursue damages against their Uncle and provided endorsed copies of declarations from said Heirs. The Court stated that it was inclined to award the difference in the amount recovered and the amount taken, as well as Attorney's fees and costs. The hearing to determine the amount of double damages was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Petition To Establish Estate'S Claim Of Ownership To Property Misappropriated By Former Administrator Sam David, And For Order Directing Its Transfer Back To Estate, And For The Imposition Of Double Damages Against Sam David
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Deborah Fox for and with Barbara Simon.

The Court addressed the petition to establish estate's claim of ownership to property misappropriated by former administrator noting that the former administrator had absconded with over $1,000,000.00 in his own name and that a previous order had been signed by Judge Sing holding the account. Ms. Fox confirmed that there is $850,000.00 held in a frozen bank account, that they believe that the former administrator may have monies in accounts at Bank of America or at Schwab. The Court expressed some concern with regard to it having jurisdiction over the bank for the transfer. Ms. Fox stated that she had spoken with the legal branch of BofA and that they stated that if the Court sets forth an order that they would follow it. Discussions were held with regard to service on BofA and Schwab, whether the Court would have jurisdiction to order the transfer, judgment entering for a specific amount, the writ of attachment, how the interest would be calulated by the bank, and the language of the proposed order. Ms. Fox confirmed that the bank does not have a problem with the language in the proposed order, that there are no issues with jurisdiction, and that no one from the bank will be appearing. Ms. Fox also presented argument to support the request for the double damages noting that if there were any extraordinary fees that they should be awarded against the former administrator. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Jun-27-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Attorney Shelley Feinberg appeared by telephone.

The Court stated that it received the Waiver of Notice signed by the Bonding Company and that there appears to be no objections. The Court also noted that the hearing date of July 30, 2013 on the Petition to Establish Estate's Claim of Ownership to Property, etc. is the only future date on calendar and explained to Ms. Feinberg that the bond for Mr. David can not be exonerated. Ms. Feinberg explained that she will not be able to do a final accounting because she has not been provided with any documents. The Court granted the Amended Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
